# Research - 2026-07-22

Focus: **all** (CLI, providers, tools, MCP, commands, hooks, skills, plugins)

> Wednesday, `date -u +%u` = 3. Weekly repos (claude-code, aider) skipped per
> cadence rule. Cline shipped MODERATE-HIGH desktop features (desktop
> login-shell PATH resolution for macOS #12429, session rebuild on provider
> switch #12454, agentic compaction default #12317, updater + signed releases
> #12420) plus cross-cutting improvements (opentui 0.1.102 -> 0.4.3 upgrade
> #12453, stalled-stream watchdog #12249, auth metadata in telemetry #12274).
> Kilocode shows JetBrains rules settings parity (#12437), stalled-stream
> watchdog rewire (#12438), openrouter model resolution fix (#12430), and
> gateway optimistic tools support (#12426). OpenCode shows nested slash
> command autocomplete (#38097), draggable project rows (#38055), and Mistral
> tool call ID normalization (#37982). Zero open `auto-implement` issues
> (unchanged). One MODERATE portable item: #1 (optional tree-sitter grammars,
> ~67MB install size, conditional on making definitions tool lazy-load
> grammars). The rest are desktop-specific UI/infra (PATH resolution, signed
> releases, updater, provider session rebuild), opentui major bump (breaking
> changes risk), or watchdog re-land (already proposed yesterday).

## Executive Summary

- **#1 (MODERATE, ~5h, deps)**: Cline/Kilocode #12249 / #12438 re-land
  stalled-stream watchdog (async generator return() preemption fix).
  Yesterday's brief (#2026-07-21, item #3, MODERATE, ~4h, tool) already
  covered shell resolution rationalization. Today's Kilocode #12438 is the
  *test* rewire (post-v1.17.4 module paths) of the *same* watchdog PR
  originally reverted, so the production fix has shipped. Alexi has
  `src/core/streamingOrchestrator.ts` for streaming; check if abort/cancel
  mid-stream hangs on stalled provider responses (SAP AI Core SDK streams via
  async generators). If yes, port the watchdog pattern (hand-rolled
  AsyncIterator whose return() runs immediately and forwards to source's
  return() without awaiting outstanding pull). MODERATE value (prevents hung
  sessions on stalled streams; improves reliability). Estimated ~5h: review
  streamingOrchestrator abort handling (~1h), implement hand-rolled
  AsyncIterator if needed (~2h), test with mocked stalled stream (~1.5h),
  update docs (~30m). Conditional on confirming Alexi has the same async
  generator return() preemption issue.
- **#2 (MODERATE, ~4h, deps)**: Tree-sitter grammars (~67MB) are hard
  dependencies but only used by definitions tool (`src/tool/tools/definitions.ts`).
  Port Cline #12379's pattern: move `tree-sitter-typescript`,
  `tree-sitter-javascript`, `tree-sitter-bash` to optional peerDependencies,
  lazy-load via dynamic import in `src/context/treeSitter.ts`, surface clear
  error when grammars are missing ("`npm install tree-sitter-typescript
  tree-sitter-javascript tree-sitter-bash` to enable definitions tool").
  MODERATE value (saves ~67MB install size for users who never call
  definitions tool; aligns with optional-dep hygiene from yesterday's #1).
  Estimated ~4h: move grammars to optional peerDependencies (~30m), refactor
  `src/context/treeSitter.ts` to dynamic import (~1.5h: lazy-load parsers,
  add try/catch, surface errors), test definitions tool with/without grammars

- **#6 (SKIP, release infra)**: Cline #12420 adds auto-update via Tauri

- **#7 (SKIP, telemetry metadata)**: Cline #12274 / #12444 add auth metadata

- **#8 (SKIP, agentic compaction default)**: Cline #12317 defaults to agentic

- **Rotation**: kilocode 0 dryness (active: stalled-stream watchdog test
  rewire, rules settings, openrouter fix), cline 0 dryness (active: desktop
  PATH resolution, provider session rebuild, opentui upgrade, updater,
  watchdog re-land), opencode 4 dryness (nested slash autocomplete, draggable

## Detailed Findings

#### 1. Stalled-stream watchdog (async generator return() preemption) (tool, ~5h, MODERATE)

- **What**: Cline #12249 prevents stalled agent streams by replacing async
  generator with hand-rolled AsyncIterator whose return() runs immediately
  and forwards to source's return() without awaiting outstanding pull. Async
  generators' return() cannot preempt an in-flight internal await; when
  suspended mid-await it only applies once that await settles on its own. For
  a genuinely stalled stream that await never settles, so interrupting a
  session mid-stream (e.g. aborting while a local tool call is pending) hung
  instead of cancelling. The PR replaces the generator with a hand-rolled
  AsyncIterator whose return() runs immediately and forwards to the source's
  return() without waiting on any outstanding pull, matching how interruption
  already behaves for the unwrapped upstream iterator. Also makes stream
  timeout tool-aware (long-running tools like bash detach extend the idle
  window so the watchdog doesn't interrupt a legitimately blocked stream).
  Kilocode #12438 re-wires the test after v1.17.4 compat removed/moved the
  modules the test imported (RepositoryCache, Ripgrep now in
  @opencode-ai/core). The production fix shipped; #12438 is just the test
  re-land.
- **Why**: Alexi has `src/core/streamingOrchestrator.ts` for streaming chat
  completion and `src/providers/sapOrchestration.ts` which streams via SAP AI
  SDK async generators. Check if abort/cancel mid-stream hangs on stalled
  provider responses (SAP AI Core SDK streams via async generators, same
  pattern as Cline). If yes, port the watchdog pattern: replace async
  generator with hand-rolled AsyncIterator whose return() runs immediately
  and forwards to the source's return() without awaiting outstanding pull.
  MODERATE value (prevents hung sessions on stalled streams; improves
  reliability when provider responses stall mid-turn or user aborts). Note:
  yesterday's brief (2026-07-21, item #3, MODERATE, ~4h, tool) covered shell
  resolution rationalization (#12331). Today's #12249 / #12438 is a
  *different* fix (stalled streams, not shell detection). Estimated ~5h:
  review `src/core/streamingOrchestrator.ts` abort handling (~1h: check if
  async generator return() is used, identify stall risk), implement
  hand-rolled AsyncIterator if needed (~2h: replace generator with
  AsyncIterator, forward return() immediately, add idle watchdog if not
  present), test with mocked stalled stream (~1.5h: mock stalled SAP response,
  verify abort cancels immediately), update docs (~30m: document watchdog
  timeout and tool-aware idle window). Conditional on confirming Alexi has
  the same async generator return() preemption issue.
- **Source**: cline/cline PR #12249 "fix(cli): prevent stalled agent streams"
  (merged 2026-07-22T04:12Z, commit `8f3a9d2`). Full PR body at
  https://github.com/cline/cline/pull/12249. Kilocode PR #12438 "fix(cli):
  rewire stalled-stream watchdog test to post-v1.17.4 modules" (merged
  2026-07-22T09:32Z, commit `5b8c1e3`). Full PR body at
  https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode/pull/12438.
- **Category**: tool. **Size**: moderate (~5h: review streamingOrchestrator,
  implement AsyncIterator if needed, test with mocked stall, update docs).
- **Status**: MODERATE (prevents hung sessions on stalled streams; improves
  reliability; conditional on confirming Alexi has the same async generator
  return() preemption issue — SAP AI SDK uses async generators for streaming,
  so risk is present).

#### 2. Optional tree-sitter grammar dependencies (deps, ~4h, MODERATE)

- **What**: Tree-sitter grammars (`tree-sitter-typescript`,
  `tree-sitter-javascript`, `tree-sitter-bash`) are hard dependencies totaling
  ~67MB installed size. They are only used by the definitions tool
  (`src/tool/tools/definitions.ts` and `src/context/treeSitter.ts`). Users who
  never call definitions tool still pay the install cost. Port Cline #12379's
  pattern (optional provider peer deps): move grammars to optional
  peerDependencies, lazy-load via dynamic import in
  `src/context/treeSitter.ts`, surface clear error when grammars are missing
  ("`npm install tree-sitter-typescript tree-sitter-javascript
  tree-sitter-bash` to enable definitions tool"). The definitions tool already
  has a fallback regex-based extractor for TypeScript/JavaScript/Python/Bash
  (see `definitions.ts:48-75`), but tree-sitter provides more accurate symbol
  extraction for complex codebases. Making grammars optional preserves
  definitions tool functionality via regex fallback while reducing install
  size for users who never use it or prefer the regex path.
- **Why**: Yesterday's brief (2026-07-21, item #1, HIGH, ~6h, deps) proposed
  auditing `package.json` for large optional dependencies following Cline
  #12379 (optional provider peer deps saved ~355MB). Tree-sitter grammars are
  the largest feature-gated dependencies in Alexi (~67MB: tree-sitter-typescript
  43MB, tree-sitter-bash 17MB, tree-sitter-javascript 4.4MB, tree-sitter core
  2.9MB). Puppeteer (~136K) is already in optionalDependencies. Making
  grammars optional saves ~67MB install size for users who never call
  definitions tool (or prefer regex-based extraction). MODERATE value (reduces
  install size by ~14% of current 490MB node_modules; aligns with
  optional-dep hygiene from yesterday's #1; users only pay for what they use).
  Estimated ~4h: move grammars to optional peerDependencies (~30m: edit
  package.json, move tree-sitter-* from dependencies to
  optionalDependencies, keep as devDependencies for CI), refactor
  `src/context/treeSitter.ts` to dynamic import (~1.5h: lazy-load parsers via
  `await import('tree-sitter-typescript')` in try/catch, surface 'grammars
  not installed' error with npm install command, fall back to regex extractor
  in definitions.ts when grammars are missing), test definitions tool
  with/without grammars (~1.5h: fresh install without grammars, verify regex
  fallback works, install grammars and verify tree-sitter path works), update
  docs (~30m: document optional grammars and how to install).
- **Source**: Inspired by cline/cline PR #12379 "feat(llms): make Claude Code
  and Codex provider packages optional peers" (merged 2026-07-21T05:27Z,
  commit `8a9e1f3`). Full PR body at
  https://github.com/cline/cline/pull/12379. Alexi's tree-sitter usage
  identified via `grep 'import.*tree-sitter' src/**/*.ts` (9 matches:
  `src/context/treeSitter.ts` imports Parser, TypeScript, JavaScript, Bash;
  `src/context/symbols.ts` uses tree-sitter types).
- **Category**: deps. **Size**: moderate (~4h: move to optional
  peerDependencies, lazy-load, test with/without, update docs).
- **Status**: MODERATE (saves ~67MB install size, ~14% of node_modules;
  aligns with optional-dep hygiene; users only pay for what they use; regex
  fallback preserves definitions tool functionality).

#### 3. Other non-portable / low-signal commits

- Cline #12429 "fix(desktop): resolve login shell PATH so agent can find gh
  and other CLI tools": Desktop-specific (macOS Tauri app launched from
  Finder/Dock inherits launchd's minimal PATH, needs to resolve login shell
  PATH at sidecar startup). Alexi is CLI-first (launched from terminal with
  full PATH). Skip.
- Cline #12454 "fix(desktop): rebuild sessions when switching providers":
  Desktop-specific (recreate sessions with existing transcript when switching
  providers, e.g. OpenAI -> Anthropic). Alexi uses SAP AI Core (single
  provider via `routing-config.json`); model switching does not cross provider
  boundaries. Skip (not applicable to single-provider architecture).
- Cline #12420 "feat(desktop): auto-updates + automated signed releases from
  GitHub Actions": Desktop-specific (Tauri updater, signed DMGs, release
  workflow). Alexi is npm CLI, not Tauri desktop app. Skip.
- Cline #12453 "feat(cli): upgrade opentui 0.1.102 -> 0.4.3": Major bump to
  opentui (TUI framework). Alexi uses Ink 7.1.1 (React 19), not opentui.
  Skip.
- Cline #12317 "feat(core): default to agentic compaction": Defaults to
  agentic compaction strategy (LLM-driven context summarization) instead of
  basic (simple truncation). Alexi has `src/core/compaction.ts` for context
  window management. Review to see if compaction strategy is configurable and
  if agentic is an option. LOW value (compaction strategy tuning is not a
  feature request from users; defaulting to agentic requires LLM calls for
  every compaction, increasing cost). Skip.
- Cline #12274, #12444 "Add auth metadata to the auth telemetry": Adds
  session duration, request ID to telemetry events. Alexi has
  `src/utils/telemetry.ts` (local-only, "For now, we just store locally").
  Skip (telemetry is not production-connected; adding metadata is low-value
  without a telemetry backend).
- Cline #12424, #12425, #12426, #12427, #12428, #12432, #12434: Desktop app
  UI (account page functional, MCP server dialog simplification, settings
  alignment, thinking indicator, diff view actions, open-in-editor picker).
  Alexi is CLI-first. Skip.
- Cline #12401 "fix(cli): keep status delivery failures non-fatal": Error
  handling fix (status delivery failures no longer crash sessions). Alexi has
  `src/core/sessionManager.ts` for session lifecycle. Review to see if status
  delivery is present and if failures are fatal. LOW signal (error handling
  edge case).
- Cline #12445 "fix(deps): bump mermaid to 11.16.0 and protobufjs to 7.6.5":
  Security CVEs (mermaid CVE-2026-41148/41149/41150/41159, protobufjs

- Cline #12407 "Revert 'docs: add Kimi K3 to ClinePass documentation'": Model
  catalog revert. Alexi uses SAP AI Core, not ClinePass. Skip.
- Kilocode #12440, #12437 "feat(jetbrains): add Rules settings page":
  JetBrains IDE plugin feature (rules UI parity with CLI). Alexi is CLI-first,
  no IDE plugin. Skip.
- Kilocode #12430 "fix(cli): allow openrouter models in routed model
  resolution": OpenRouter model resolution fix. Alexi uses SAP AI Core, not
  OpenRouter. Skip.
- Kilocode #12426 "fix(gateway): optimistically assume tools support when
  supported_parameters is missing": Gateway fix (assume tools support when
  metadata is missing). Alexi has `src/providers/` for provider abstraction.
  Review to see if SAP AI Core provider checks supported_parameters for tools
  support. LOW signal (edge case handling).
- Kilocode #12405 "chore(ci): trim darwin test profile": CI optimization

- OpenCode #38248, #38080, #38186, #38097, #38101, #38055, #37986, #37982,

## Actionable Recommendations

1. **Priority order**: One MODERATE item (#1 stalled-stream watchdog,
   conditional on confirming Alexi has async generator return() preemption
   issue), one MODERATE item (#2 optional tree-sitter grammars, immediate),
   six SKIP items (#3 desktop PATH, #4 opentui bump, #5 provider session
   rebuild, #6 updater, #7 telemetry metadata, #8 agentic compaction). #2 is
   the highest-value item today: saves ~67MB install size (~14% of
   node_modules) by making tree-sitter grammars optional peerDependencies
   (aligns with yesterday's #1 optional-dep hygiene). #1 is MODERATE but
   conditional: requires confirming SAP AI SDK streams have the same async
   generator return() preemption issue as Cline (likely true since both use
   async generators for streaming).
2. **Optional tree-sitter grammars (#2)**: Surface as an issue. Action: Move
   `tree-sitter-typescript`, `tree-sitter-javascript`, `tree-sitter-bash` to
   optional peerDependencies (keep as devDependencies for CI), lazy-load via
   dynamic import in `src/context/treeSitter.ts`, surface clear error when
   grammars are missing ("`npm install tree-sitter-typescript
   tree-sitter-javascript tree-sitter-bash` to enable definitions tool"), test
   definitions tool with/without grammars (regex fallback should work when
   grammars are absent). Reference: Cline #12379
   (https://github.com/cline/cline/pull/12379). Estimated ~4h: move to
   optional peerDependencies (~30m), refactor to dynamic import (~1.5h), test
   (~1.5h), update docs (~30m). MODERATE value.
3. **Stalled-stream watchdog (#1)**: Do NOT surface as an issue until Alexi's
   streaming abort handling is reviewed. Action: Read
   `src/core/streamingOrchestrator.ts` and
   `src/providers/sapOrchestration.ts` to see if (1) streaming uses async
   generators (SAP AI SDK likely does), (2) abort/cancel mid-stream hangs on
   stalled provider responses, (3) async generator return() is used for
   cleanup. If abort hangs, implement watchdog following Cline's pattern:
   replace async generator with hand-rolled AsyncIterator whose return() runs
   immediately and forwards to source's return() without awaiting outstanding
   pull, add idle watchdog with tool-aware timeout (long-running tools extend
   idle window). Reference: Cline #12249
   (https://github.com/cline/cline/pull/12249). Estimated ~5h if needed.
   MODERATE value (conditional).
4. **Desktop-specific (#3, #5, #6)**: Do NOT surface as issues. Cline #12429
   (login shell PATH), #12454 (provider session rebuild), #12420 (updater)
   are desktop app features. Alexi is CLI-first. Skip.
5. **Opentui major bump (#4)**: Do NOT surface as an issue. Cline #12453
   upgrades opentui (breaking changes). Alexi uses Ink 7.1.1, not opentui.
   Skip.
6. **Telemetry metadata (#7)**: Do NOT surface as an issue. Cline #12274 /
   #12444 add telemetry metadata. Alexi's telemetry is local-only (not
   production-connected). Skip (low value without telemetry backend).
7. **Agentic compaction default (#8)**: Do NOT surface as an issue. Cline
   #12317 defaults to agentic compaction. Alexi has
   `src/core/compaction.ts`. Review if compaction strategy is configurable.
   LOW value (not a user feature request; increases cost). Skip.
8. **Rotation**: kilocode 0 dryness (active: stalled-stream watchdog test
   rewire, rules settings, openrouter fix), cline 0 dryness (active: desktop

9. **Observation**: Zero open `auto-implement` issues (unchanged). The

10. **Carry-forward from yesterday (2026-07-21)**: Yesterday's brief
